help.overview: The Sensors menu is where you pick the ground station the rest of the app calculates from. Sensors are grouped by the network they primarily support, with each entry showing the sensor's name on the left and its owning country or organization on the right. The selected sensor drives look angles, field-of-view cones, watchlist pass alerts, and every other sensor-aware menu.
help.imgAlt: The Sensors menu showing grouped sensor networks
help.imgCaption: Sensors are grouped by network. Click a group header to select the whole network at once.
help.sensorTypes:
• Phased-array radars: wide, fast scanning; typically limited to Low Earth Orbit (LEO).
  • Mechanical radars: steerable dishes usable against both LEO and Geostationary Orbit (GEO).
  • Optical telescopes: primarily used for GEO and deep space; they only work at night in clear skies, while radars work day and night.
  • TT&C antennas: steerable communication dishes (DSN, ESTRACK, SCN, Galileo) that track cooperative satellites via their radio downlink; they cannot detect uncooperative objects or debris.
help.howToUse:
1. Open the menu from the bottom bar or press S.
  2. Click an individual sensor to select it. The camera flies to its location.
  3. Or click a group header (e.g. "All Space Surveillance Network Sensors") to select the entire network, which is great for visualizing coverage.
  4. Use Reset Sensor to clear the selection.
help.tip1: Many calculations (like Look Angles) need a single sensor. If you selected a whole network, use the Multi-Site Looks menu instead or pick one sensor at a time.
help.tip2: Every sensor is also available from the command palette: type the sensor's name to switch without opening this menu.
help.tip3: Sensor parameters come from publicly available data; you can review them in the Sensor Info menu. Corrections are welcome at admin@keeptrack.space.
help.shortcutCamera: Snap the camera to the current sensor
